data.epi_content:
A somewhat long day, or rather than long, just a tiring one, was coming to an end.

After being cooped up in my room for days, I experienced being burned by fire for the first time in my life, tried consuming white phosphorus matches—which were supposedly only used in the old days—in a different way than the little match girl by directly ingesting them orally rather than through my bronchi, and had a pleasant conversation with Evan.

Well, there was no shouting, and neither of us intentionally said hurtful things to each other, so I suppose that counts as pleasant.

In my memories, the conversations between Evan and this young lady before she locked herself in her room out of sadness consisted only of vicious words meant to dig into each other's hearts, as if wishing the other would just die.

Anyway, it was quite an eventful day.

It was only natural that my body felt tired after so many things happening in a single day. I dragged my heavy body, quickly washed my face, and rather than lying down on the bed, I collapsed onto it.

"Urghhhh."

I turned my body to look at the ceiling once, closed my eyes, and wanted to sleep in a proper position, but I didn't have the energy to move a finger.

I wonder if I might suffocate to death with my face buried in the pillow like this.

Suffocating because I'm too tired to get up after lying face down—it might not be enough to win a Darwin Award, but it could at least make me a nominee.

I give up completely and relax my muscles to fall asleep with my face buried in the pillow.

And then I suddenly had a dream.

To summarize the content briefly, it seemed that the time I spent here was just a somewhat peculiar nightmare, as I woke up in my own room where I always stayed.

In my room with thick black curtains blocking the sunlight from the windows and an incredibly comfortable bed made of two mattresses stacked together.

Thinking I'd had a strange dream, I left my room to refresh myself and took out some oolong tea from the refrigerator in the living room, adding plenty of ice.

I swirled the cup three or four times to make the tea cold, then inserted a plastic straw with Mickey Mouse on it.

And surprisingly, when I took a sip, it had no taste at all.

Feeling annoyed, I repeatedly took sips of tea, held it in my mouth, and spat it out, but that didn't make any non-existent taste appear.

I couldn't help but cry at the feeling of sinking endlessly.

A grown man crying just because tea had no taste.

A man?

I'm just a small, powerless girl, haha.

Was that so? That's how it was.

Come to think of it, the calluses that used to be on my fingertips were gone, and my hands were soft like when I used to play the piano.

My skin was pale white, and long white hair fell over my shoulders.

When I looked in the mirror, a girl with a cold face was looking back at me, tears streaming down her face.

That expressionless face, devoid of any emotion, was the expression I always wore.

After staring into her eyes for a while, the corners of her mouth turned up and the girl smiled faintly.

Only her mouth moved to smile.

Just slightly lifting the corners of your mouth is enough to smile, isn't it?

...

I wake up in the morning, get myself ready as usual, grab a book to read, and head to the classroom.

The atmosphere in the classroom that needs no explanation, and Lydia's bullying.

This life that seems to repeat itself every day, perhaps painfully, feels endless like Sisyphus's punishment.

I struggle through the day, sleep, wake up, and see no improvement whatsoever—today is the same as yesterday, and the future will be the same as today.

So what meaning should I find here?

Should I positively accept that enduring this lethargic life every day is the minimum resistance of a person like me?

Anyway, today went by rather lightly.

All that Lydia and her group did to me was just physical violence.

Maybe they only committed light violence like kicking and slapping.

Anyway, my pride wasn't particularly hurt, I wasn't sexually humiliated, and I didn't collapse buried in self-loathing.

I can't know for sure.

I must be fine since I'm okay now.
data.epi_content2:
Though I did fall asleep from exhaustion after the bullying.

It's not really important to elaborate on how much I suffered.

The important thing is that I had another dream.

Ridiculously, it was the same dream I had before waking up in the morning—the dream of drinking oolong tea.

Actually, I euphemistically said I fell asleep, but I merely passed out after being hit.

I'm so weak that I might lose consciousness just from one slap to the face.

Perhaps someone thought I might die if left lying on the bare floor.

Someone must have carried me, as I was sitting properly in a chair, slumped over on the desk.

Thanks to sleeping—or rather, collapsing—with my back bent, my entire back from waist to shoulders feels stiff.

As I stretch and get up, the children around me look at me.

Of course, they just glance at me like I'm some rare animal and then turn away.

Getting back to the point, do I want to drink tea, or do I miss the reality where I could leave my room, throw on some sweatpants, and buy a cup of oolong tea at a café just five minutes away?

It could be neither, or it could be both.

Well, even if it's just a vain fantasy now, these thoughts that occasionally come to mind breathe life into me.

At least now, very unfortunately, the reality is that I'm attending this shitty Academy, carrying all sorts of doubts in my head.

A life where I could peel tangerines without worry in a warm room, drink coffee, and read novels while laughing carelessly—that's just imagination now.

Let's forget it. Remembering only brings pain.

Just as I was being pessimistic about the empty repetition of every day as usual, someone spoke to me.

"Excuse me, Miss Erica."

Miss. That's something I rarely hear these days.

Lydia, who used to wipe her feet on me, calls me "miss" to mock me, and Vivian, with her beautiful eyes slightly sparkling with admiration, calls me "miss" as always.

And now it seems the only people who call me with such a warm voice are those I dislike.

When I turned my head, sure enough, it was Vivian.

"Hello!"

When our eyes met, Vivian greeted me with a cheerful demeanor.

"...Nice to meet you."

Not that nice, but it's just a courtesy phrase, so what can I do?

If I say it's nice, then it's nice.

"Why are you looking for me again today?"

"You said you'd come to class last time, so I just wanted to see you!

And I have something I want to tell you."

Saying that, she fumbled in her bosom and took something out.

It was cookies.

"Ah, I baked again. This time they're just milk cookies."

"...Hmm, thank you. I'll enjoy them."

If I were to hear it cynically, it sounds like she came to check if I was actually attending class properly.

Thinking that while looking at Vivian's face with a crooked expression, I just let out a small sigh.

Well, who could think such thoughts while looking at that innocent yet beautiful face?

In fact, from the moment I received the sweet cookies, most of my doubts could be overlooked.

One fortunate thing about falling into this world is that even someone like me can eat cookies full of sweet sugar because slaves who are considered even more worthless than me, not even treated as people, are working hard to make sugar, making their black skin even hotter.

"I told you in my room last time.

We are not that kind of relationship."
data.epi_content3:
While drawing a firm line asking her not to cross it, I still hold tightly to the cookies I received.

Hearing my words, Vivian thought hard before speaking.

"I know Evan likes me, not as a friend but romantically."

"So? Did you come to mock me?

I told you last time, Evan and I..."

I started to continue but suddenly became unclear.

When I said I had no feelings for Evan, until then I felt somewhat separated from this pale young lady, but now I feel half-mixed with her, and even now we're merging together.

Quite unpleasant, but, ha.

"But I don't like that our relationship has become strained because of this.

I want to be close with you, miss. Of course, I want to be close with Evan too, but... I don't know."

This is completely like she's already rejected him in her heart.

"Actually, I'm not sure yet who I like, who I dislike, who I love, things like that."

Or not.

Yes, you're young.

Your body may be fully grown, but your heart is as pure as a child's.

Perhaps that's why men flock around you.

Thinking about it that way, it seems a bit dangerous.

A romance with handsome men could turn into a thriller targeting a mentally immature person with an adult body.

"So why are you telling me this?

I don't particularly like revealing my innermost thoughts straightforwardly."

I prefer to just let things pass even when we both know.

Maybe Evan feels the same way.

He didn't bring up anything and just smoothly moved past the conversation.

"Well, did you start disliking Evan?"

"Ah, no, that's not it... but even if I like him, I don't think it's that kind of like."

In other words, it sounds like she likes Evan but not romantically.

Is this another common cliché? Not being aware of your own feelings and then suddenly starting to recognize them.

Maybe Evan isn't the male protagonist.

Maybe he's just a supporting character like me, something reusable multiple times rather than just once.

How ridiculous must I have seemed to Vivian?

This girl who has nothing better than her status must have seemed so ridiculous.

Though she wouldn't laugh at me.

I'm well aware that this is just a vain delusion born from an inferiority complex.

But it's unfair.

Some people desperately want, envy, and even jealously covet a love they can never obtain, while others get it so easily.

Well, you're the protagonist, so maybe it's nothing special to you.

"So what did you want to say?"

"Would you... have meals with me like before, and with Evan too when he has time...?"

I must have been wearing a cold expression because when our eyes met, she faltered slightly and lowered her gaze as she spoke.

Why does this beautiful girl approach me?

If she's such a brilliantly shining protagonist, I wish she would just glance at me at the bottom and discard me.

Why do you have to drag me out?

It only makes me feel more miserable.

I slightly opened my mouth, then closed it, and just smiled.
